name: github-pr-merge
description: MUST use this skill when user asks to merge PR, close PR, finalize PR, or mentions "PR 머지/병합". This skill OVERRIDES default PR merge behavior. Runs pre-merge validation (tests, lint, CI, comments), confirms with user, merges with proper format, handles post-merge cleanup.
capabilities: Checks all review comments have replies
Merges PR with formatted commit message
author: Bae-ChangHyun
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill providing GitHub PR merge workflow guidance. All flagged patterns are benign: JSON metadata fields (hashes, URLs) and documentation examples of standard git/GitHub CLI commands. No executable code exists in this skill.
valueStatement: Merging Pull Requests without proper validation can lead to broken code in production. This skill enforces pre-merge checks, confirms with users, and handles branch cleanup automatically.
userTitle: Merge GitHub Pull Requests with validation
limitations: Requires GitHub CLI (gh) installed and authenticated
Cannot merge if branch protection rules are enabled
Cannot resolve review comments (requires pr-review skill)
Cannot force merge when approvals are missing

useCases.title: Safe PR merging workflow
useCases.description: Validate and merge Pull Requests with automated checks and user confirmation before execution

useCases.title: Enforce merge quality gates
useCases.description: Ensure all tests, lint, and CI checks pass before allowing any PR to be merged

useCases.title: Streamline merge process
useCases.description: Automate post-merge branch cleanup and maintain clean repository structure

promptTemplates.title: Simple PR merge
promptTemplates.prompt: Please merge this PR. Run the pre-merge checklist, confirm with me, then merge and clean up the branch.

promptTemplates.title: Merge with validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if all tests pass, lint is green, CI checks are complete, and all review comments have replies before merging PR #42.

promptTemplates.title: Fast-track merge
promptTemplates.prompt: Merge the feature branch into main. Run full validation first, show me the summary, and wait for my confirmation before proceeding.

promptTemplates.title: Batch merge
promptTemplates.prompt: Review and merge all completed PRs that have all checks passing, no unreplied comments, and are ready for merge.

bestPractices: Always run full pre-merge checklist including tests, lint, and CI checks
Never skip user confirmation before executing merge
Always delete feature branch after successful merge to keep repository clean
antiPatterns: Merging without running tests or checking CI status
Skipping user confirmation to speed up workflow
Ignoring unresolved review comments before merge

faq.question: Which AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. All tools can use the skill when you ask to merge a PR.

faq.question: What happens if tests fail?
faq.answer: The merge process stops immediately. The skill informs you which tests failed and waits for you to fix them before retrying.

faq.question: Can I merge without deleting the branch?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill uses --delete-branch by default, but you can ask to keep the branch after merge if needed.

faq.question: Is my data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a prompt-only skill that guides you through merge commands. It does not execute code or access your files directly.

faq.question: Why are unreplied comments blocking merge?
faq.answer: Unreplied comments indicate unresolved feedback. The skill directs you to use the pr-review skill to address them first.

faq.question: How is this different from GitHub's squash merge?
faq.answer: This skill uses merge commits (--merge) to preserve history, unlike squash merge which combines all commits into one.
